summary: The docker build (buildx bake) failed because a RUN step attempted to wget a prebuilt nydus binary from GitHub and the HTTP request returned 502, causing the build to abort.
root_cause: A network download during the Docker build failed: wget returned "HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway" when trying to fetch the nydus release tarball, and the failing command caused buildx bake to abort with exit code 1.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the HTTP 502 error returned by the wget command that failed to download the external release artifact.
Shows the exact build step that ran wget to fetch the external nydus artifact.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The build step failed with a non-zero exit code when the wget command could not complete.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The top-level buildx bake error indicates the solver failed because the wget process failed, showing this is the root failing operation.
Dockerfile line location is printed, indicating the failing RUN instruction is at Dockerfile line 336.

suggested_fix: Retry the job (transient network 502). If the failure persists, make the Dockerfile download step more robust or avoid live downloads: e.g. use wget with retries/backoff (wget --tries=5 --waitretry=5 ...), host a mirrored/cached copy of the nydus tarball, or vendor the binary into the build context / use a cached artifact so the build doesn’t depend on a single external HTTP fetch.

summary: unsupported_context_too_large: context (896814 chars) exceeds shim cap (480000)
One Jest test failed: a config 'get' test expected a registry URL without a trailing slash but received the same URL with a trailing slash.
root_cause: The test 'pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc' failed because the command output returned "https://my-org.registry.example.com/" (with a trailing slash) while the test expected "https://my-org.registry.example.com" (no trailing slash).
relevant_tests: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: The test expectation (registry URL without trailing slash).
Actual value returned by the code (registry URL with trailing slash), which caused the assertion mismatch.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Summary that the failing test caused the job to fail.

suggested_fix: Either normalize the registry URL returned by the code (trim a trailing slash before printing/returning it) or update the test to accept both forms (e.g., compare after trimming trailing slashes or use a URL-normalization helper).
